source_inputs: Copia de Prueba tecnica - Fullstack Engineer.md

context_scan.repository_state: No application scaffold found yet. Workspace currently contains challenge and research documents only.
context_scan.detected_stack: No package.json, pyproject.toml, requirements file, app directory, src directory, api directory, db directory, or existing specs were present before this feature spec.
runtime_constraints: Challenge requires a backend service for compliance obligations.
Challenge expects backend domain rules to be isolated from HTTP and persistence.
Challenge accepts PostgreSQL as recommended persistence and SQLite as acceptable fallback, but this functional spec does not choose implementation details.
context_scan.test_setup_available: No test framework is configured yet.

scope.product_goal: Provide the backend behavior needed to track company compliance obligations with reliable state transitions, document-gated submission, masked sensitive data, overdue and due-soon calculation, audit history, and concurrency-safe status changes.
in_scope: Create, read, update, and delete obligations.
Expose an operation to change an obligation status.
Validate all status transitions according to the allowed workflow.
Prevent submission when an obligation requires a document and no attached document exists.
Treat overdue and due soon as derived information, not as persisted workflow statuses.
Calculate both overdue and due soon in the backend from the same domain rules and due-soon configuration.
Accept the full company tax id on write while returning only a masked representation on read.
Record an audit event for every successful status change.
Expose obligation detail with current state, masked tax id, derived overdue flag, derived due-soon flag, valid next transitions, submission blocking reason when applicable, and status audit history.
Handle concurrent status changes so stale requests cannot silently overwrite newer changes.
Return consistent, machine-readable errors for not found, invalid transition, document-required submission, validation failure, and version conflict.
out_of_scope: Real file upload or binary document storage.
Tenant permissions and role-based access control.
Advanced search, pagination, and saved filters.
Full audit of every field edit.
PII key rotation or advanced secret-management flows.

domain_language.obligation: A compliance task owned by a company or responsible person, with a due date, workflow status, optional document requirement, and sensitive company tax id.
domain_language.document_required: A business rule indicating that a supporting document must exist before the obligation can be submitted.
domain_language.attached_document: Metadata that represents the existence of a document for the obligation. The first scope may mock the file itself.
domain_language.overdue: A derived condition meaning the due date has passed and the obligation is not submitted or done.
domain_language.due_soon: A derived condition meaning the obligation is still active and its due date falls within the configured upcoming-due window.
domain_language.due_soon_window: A configurable number of calendar days used by the backend to decide whether an active obligation is due soon.
domain_language.audit_event: An immutable record of a successful status change.
domain_language.expected_version: The version the caller believes the obligation currently has when requesting a status change.

use_cases.name: Create obligation
use_cases.description: The client creates an obligation with required descriptive fields, due date, owner, document requirement flag, and full company tax id.
use_cases.success_outcome: The obligation is stored and subsequent reads expose safe, derived, and display-ready fields without leaking the raw company tax id.

use_cases.name: List obligations for dashboard
use_cases.description: The dashboard requests obligations and receives enough information to show status, due dates, overdue highlighting, due-soon highlighting, upcoming obligations, and masked tax id display.
use_cases.success_outcome: The client can render dashboard data without calculating domain-critical date states or status transitions itself.

use_cases.name: View obligation detail
use_cases.description: The detail view requests one obligation and receives all user-facing fields, derived overdue state, derived due-soon state, available next transitions, submission blocking information, and status history.
use_cases.success_outcome: The client can render permitted actions and explain blocked submission without duplicating backend domain rules.

use_cases.name: Change obligation status
use_cases.description: The client requests a status change with the target status and the version it last read.
use_cases.success_outcome: A valid transition is applied, the obligation version advances, and an audit event is recorded.

use_cases.name: Reject invalid status transition
use_cases.description: The client requests a status change that is not allowed by the workflow.
use_cases.success_outcome: The change is rejected, the obligation remains unchanged, and no audit event is recorded.

use_cases.name: Block document-gated submission
use_cases.description: The client tries to submit an obligation that requires a document but has no attached document metadata.
use_cases.success_outcome: The submission is rejected, the obligation remains unchanged, and the response identifies the document requirement.

use_cases.name: Handle concurrent status changes
use_cases.description: Two clients attempt to change the same obligation based on the same previous version.
use_cases.success_outcome: Only the first valid change succeeds. The stale request receives a conflict and must refresh before retrying.

use_cases.name: Read status audit history
use_cases.description: The detail view displays each successful status change in chronological order.
use_cases.success_outcome: The user can inspect how the obligation moved through the workflow without exposing sensitive data.

business_rules.name: Allowed statuses
business_rules.rule: An obligation status must be one of pending, in_progress, submitted, or done.

business_rules.name: Allowed status transitions
business_rules.rule: Allowed transitions are pending to in_progress, in_progress to submitted, in_progress to pending, submitted to done, submitted to in_progress, and done to in_progress.

business_rules.name: Invalid transitions are never persisted
business_rules.rule: A status transition outside the allowed workflow must be rejected and must not change obligation state, version, or audit history.

business_rules.name: Document-gated submission
business_rules.rule: If an obligation requires a document and has no attached document metadata, it cannot transition to submitted.

business_rules.name: Overdue is derived
business_rules.rule: An obligation is overdue when its due date is before the current business date and its status is neither submitted nor done.

business_rules.name: Overdue is not a workflow state
business_rules.rule: Overdue must not appear as a persisted obligation status or as a target status for transitions.

business_rules.name: Due soon is derived
business_rules.rule: An obligation is due soon when its due date is on or after the current business date, on or before the configured due-soon limit, and its status is neither submitted nor done.

business_rules.name: Due soon uses backend configuration
business_rules.rule: The backend must use a configurable due-soon window so the client does not decide the threshold independently.

business_rules.name: Due soon is not a workflow state
business_rules.rule: Due soon must not appear as a persisted obligation status or as a target status for transitions.

business_rules.name: Sensitive tax id write behavior
business_rules.rule: The API may accept the full company tax id when creating or updating an obligation.

business_rules.name: Sensitive tax id read behavior
business_rules.rule: Read responses must never include the raw company tax id and must expose only a masked value suitable for display.

business_rules.name: Sensitive tax id logging behavior
business_rules.rule: The raw company tax id must not appear in logs, error responses, audit events, or technical metadata.

business_rules.name: Audit successful status changes
business_rules.rule: Every successful status change must produce one audit event containing the previous status, next status, change time, actor placeholder, resulting obligation version, and optional reason.

business_rules.name: No audit for failed changes
business_rules.rule: If a status change fails validation or concurrency checks, no audit event is recorded.

business_rules.name: Concurrent status changes require freshness
business_rules.rule: A status change request must identify the obligation version it was based on. If that version is stale, the request is rejected as a conflict.

business_rules.name: Frontend consumes available actions
business_rules.rule: The backend must provide enough action information for the frontend to render valid transitions and blocked submission states without recreating the state machine.

business_rules.name: Business dates
business_rules.rule: Due dates are business calendar dates, not timestamps with times of day.

business_rules.name: Owner is required
business_rules.rule: An obligation cannot exist without an owner.

acceptance_criteria.title: Create obligation with safe read response
acceptance_criteria.given: A client submits valid obligation data including a full company tax id.
acceptance_criteria.when: The obligation is created and then read.
acceptance_criteria.then: The read response includes the obligation fields, a masked company tax id, current version, derived overdue flag, derived due-soon flag, and available transitions, but never includes the raw company tax id.

acceptance_criteria.title: Reject missing owner
acceptance_criteria.given: A client submits obligation data without an owner.
acceptance_criteria.when: The create or update operation is requested.
acceptance_criteria.then: The request is rejected with a consistent validation error and no obligation is created or updated.

acceptance_criteria.title: Compute overdue for active obligations
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation has a due date before the current business date and status pending or in_progress.
acceptance_criteria.then: The response marks the obligation as overdue.

acceptance_criteria.title: Do not mark submitted or done obligations overdue
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation has a due date before the current business date and status submitted or done.
acceptance_criteria.then: The response does not mark the obligation as overdue.

acceptance_criteria.title: Compute due soon for active obligations
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation has status pending or in_progress and a due date inside the backend-configured due-soon window, including today.
acceptance_criteria.then: The response marks the obligation as due soon.

acceptance_criteria.title: Do not mark overdue obligations due soon
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation has a due date before the current business date.
acceptance_criteria.then: The response may mark it as overdue but does not mark it as due soon.

acceptance_criteria.title: Do not mark submitted or done obligations due soon
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation has a due date inside the backend-configured due-soon window and status submitted or done.
acceptance_criteria.then: The response does not mark the obligation as due soon.

acceptance_criteria.title: Due-soon threshold comes from backend configuration
acceptance_criteria.given: The backend due-soon window is configured to a specific number of calendar days.
acceptance_criteria.then: The due-soon flag is calculated using that configured window rather than any client-provided threshold.

acceptance_criteria.title: Apply valid transition
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation is pending and the client has its current version.
acceptance_criteria.when: The client requests a transition to in_progress.
acceptance_criteria.then: The status changes to in_progress, the version advances, and one audit event is visible in the obligation history.

acceptance_criteria.title: Reject invalid transition
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation is pending and the client has its current version.
acceptance_criteria.when: The client requests a transition to done.
acceptance_criteria.then: The request is rejected as an invalid status transition, the status remains pending, the version does not advance, and no audit event is created.

acceptance_criteria.title: Block submission without required document
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation is in_progress, requires a document, has no attached document metadata, and the client has its current version.
acceptance_criteria.when: The client requests a transition to submitted.
acceptance_criteria.then: The request is rejected with a document-required error, the obligation remains in_progress, and no audit event is created.

acceptance_criteria.title: Allow submission with required document present
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation is in_progress, requires a document, has attached document metadata, and the client has its current version.
acceptance_criteria.when: The client requests a transition to submitted.
acceptance_criteria.then: The status changes to submitted, the version advances, and one audit event is visible in the obligation history.

acceptance_criteria.title: Expose blocked submit reason on detail
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation is in_progress, requires a document, and has no attached document metadata.
acceptance_criteria.then: The response indicates that submission is blocked because a document is required.

acceptance_criteria.title: Expose available transitions on detail
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation exists in any valid status.
acceptance_criteria.then: The response lists the target statuses currently available according to the backend workflow and domain rules.

acceptance_criteria.title: Reject stale concurrent update
acceptance_criteria.when: The first client successfully changes the status and the second client later submits a change using expected version 3.
acceptance_criteria.then: The second request is rejected with a version conflict, the first change remains intact, and no audit event is created for the rejected request.

acceptance_criteria.title: Read audit history
acceptance_criteria.given: An obligation has multiple successful status changes.
acceptance_criteria.then: The response includes the chronological status history with from status, to status, changed time, actor placeholder, resulting version, and no raw sensitive values.

acceptance_criteria.title: Return not found consistently
acceptance_criteria.given: A client requests an obligation that does not exist.
acceptance_criteria.when: The read, update, delete, or status-change operation is requested.
acceptance_criteria.then: The response uses a consistent not-found error shape.

acceptance_criteria.title: Delete removes obligation from normal reads
acceptance_criteria.then: The obligation no longer appears in normal list or detail reads.

ux_behavior_and_integration_expectations: The frontend receives valid transitions from the backend and renders actions from that data.
The frontend receives a blocked submission reason from the backend and does not need to infer the document-gated rule.
The frontend receives masked tax id values only.
The frontend can show loading and error states using stable backend error codes.
Dashboard KPIs can be computed from backend-provided status, overdue, and due-soon information in the first scope.

open_questions.question: Should delete be hard delete for challenge simplicity or soft delete for auditability?
open_questions.default_assumption_for_next_gate: Use hard delete unless the technical spec chooses soft delete as a low-cost auditability improvement.

open_questions.question: Should document metadata be created through a dedicated document operation or embedded in obligation create/update for the first scope?
open_questions.default_assumption_for_next_gate: Prefer a small dedicated document metadata operation if it keeps the domain rule clearer.

open_questions.question: Should dashboard KPIs be served by a dedicated summary endpoint or computed by the frontend from a list response in the first scope?
open_questions.default_assumption_for_next_gate: Keep first scope simple by allowing frontend computation from list data unless performance concerns appear.

open_questions.question: What should the default due-soon window be?
open_questions.default_assumption_for_next_gate: Use 30 calendar days as a configurable default unless the user chooses a different window.
